column8: Do you use public transport? Russian cities provide a wide____________of ways to travel around, including buses. trams, and trolley-buses. (VARY)
column9: Seven of the largest Russian cities, such as Yekaterinburg, Samara and Kazan, have an underground metro system. This is very___________, especially when it's cold! (USE)
column10: For______________who want or need to fly, there are more than 1200 airports in Russia. (TRAVEL)
column11: Flights from Pulkovo Airport in Saint Petersburg often go to other countries. For this reason, the airport is often called Pulkovo_____________Airport. (NATION)
column12: Moscow is sometimes known as "the port of the five seas", due its river and canal____________to five seas, including the Black Sea and the Caspian Sea. (CONNECT)
column13: The Trans-Siberian Railway is an___________long railway. It takes more than 6 days to travel from Moscow to Vladivostok. (EXTREME)
column14: My dream finally come true. I had arrived in the truly____________city Rome. (AMAZE)
column15: Looking at the beautiful and ancient buildings everywhere, I felt so small and___________. What had I ever done to make the world a better place? (IMPORTANT)
column16: Wandering through churches, museums and galleries, I thought about all the great___________who had created the famous works of art than filled them. (ART)
column17: After a day of exploring the historic centre, I returned to my hotel____________exhausted. (TOTAL)
column18: But, I was determined to enjoy every second of my stay there. In the evening, I booked a table at a very stylish and_____________restaurant. (FASHION)
column19: As i knew it would, the view from the table on the restaurant terrace______________took my breath away. I was in padise! (ABSOLUTE)
